steps.title: Determine what the passage actually does
steps.content: The passage introduces the silversword alliance (a family of Hawaiian plants), highlights their extraordinary range of phenotypes (vines, shrubs, trees), then explains why: all descended from one tarweed ancestor that diversified as its descendants adapted to varied habitats. The passage both describes the group and explains the origin of its diversity.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'To advance the claim that all plants on the Hawaiian Islands are part of the silversword alliance and list possible ancestors...': the passage never claims all Hawaiian plants belong to the alliance, and there is one ancestor, not multiple possible ones. ✗
  • Choice B — 'To indicate the specific tarweed ancestor of all plants that grow on the Hawaiian Islands...': the tarweed is ancestor of the alliance only, not all Hawaiian plants, and the passage does not name a specific tarweed species. ✗
  • Choice C — 'To describe the silversword alliance and explain how the plant family became so varied': matches precisely — the passage describes the alliance and traces its diversity to a single ancestral tarweed adapting across habitats. ✓
  • Choice D — 'To describe the specific habitat where Argyroxiphium sandwicense are found and identify other plants that share a common ancestor with them': the passage does not describe a specific habitat for that species or identify other individual plants by name. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
